name: Apify MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that exposes thousands of Apify Actors (pre-built web scrapers and automation tools) to AI assistants, enabling structured data extraction from social media, search engines, e-commerce sites, and more.
use_cases: Extracting structured data from websites via pre-built scrapers
Social media data collection (profiles, posts, comments)
E-commerce price monitoring and product data extraction
AI agent workflows requiring web data enrichment
not_for: Users who need completely free web scraping (Apify platform has usage costs)
Simple single-page fetches where a basic HTTP client suffices
best_when: You need AI agents to access structured web data from diverse sources without writing custom scrapers, and you want a mature ecosystem of pre-built extraction tools.
avoid_when: You only need simple URL fetching, want zero platform costs, or need to run entirely offline without external service dependencies.

auth.notes: Requires APIFY_TOKEN for API access. OAuth supported for browser-based clients via mcp.apify.com. Also supports Skyfire PAY tokens for autonomous agent payments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Apify free tier includes $5/month platform credits. Individual Actor pricing varies.
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-usage based on Actor compute and platform resources. Plans from $49/month.
pricing.notes: The MCP server itself is MIT-licensed and free. Costs come from Apify platform usage when running Actors. Free tier available for light usage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses mentioned in docs. Actor tools return previews with explicit get-actor-output tool for full results, which is good agent UX. Error tracking via Sentry suggests mature error handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Actor runs are not inherently idempotent - each call triggers a new scraping run. However, output retrieval tools are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Actor tools return previews only - agents must call get-actor-output with limit/offset for full results
Dynamic tool discovery only works with some clients (VS Code, Cursor) - Claude Desktop requires manual reload
Telemetry is enabled by default - must explicitly opt out
SSE transport being sunset April 1 2026 - must use streamable HTTP
Each Actor run incurs platform costs - agents can burn through credits quickly without guardrails
Requires Apify account signup and API token before first use
